name: Pan-Fried Noodle Pillow with Stir-Fried Chinese Greens
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Bok choy, baby, sliced with flower-like bases
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------STIR-FRIED GREENS-----------------------------
1. Boil noodles in unsalted water until "al dente." Drain, flush with cold water,
then roll loosely in a lint-free towel to blot off excess water.
2. Toss noodles with sesame oil and salt, using your hands to coat and separate
the strands.
3. Heat a 12-inch well-seasoned or Silverstone heavy skillet over high heat until
hot enough to evaporate a bead of water on contact.
4. Add 5 tablespoons of oil, swirl to coat the bottom and sides of the pan,
then reduce heat to medium-high.
5. When oil is hot enough to sizzle a single noodle, coil the noodles in a pan,
then use a spatula to pack them into an even pancake or "pillow."
6. Cover and cook until the bottom is golden, about 5-7 minutes.
7. Uncover, flip the noodle pillow over with a jerk of your wrist, then cover
and cook the second side. If the pan seems dry, dribble in the remaining
tablespoon of oil around the side of the skillet before covering it.
8. Slide the noodle pillow out of the pan, cut into quarters, then garnish with
a sprinkling of black sesame seeds and vegetables.
Stir-Fried Greens:
==================
1. Soak dried mushrooms for several hours or overnight in cold water.
2. Drain, rinse, remove any stems, then cut caps into slivers (1/8-inch wide).
3. Heat a wok or a large heavy skillet over high heat until hot enough to evaporate
a bead of water on contact.
4. Add 2 tablespoons of oil, swirl to glaze the pan, then reduce heat to medium-high.
5. Add Chinese chive tops, garlic, and black mushrooms, toss until fully fragrant,
then add chanterelles, sliced scallions, and cabbage to the pan.
6. Toss just until vegetables turn supple, about 2 minutes.
7. Add baby bok choy, toss until color deepens and the vegetables are heated through,
about 20 seconds.
8. If the pan becomes too dry, dribble in a bit more oil from the side.
9. Fold in the chilis, then turn contents out onto a serving plate, either alongside
or on top of the noodle pillow.
